mentions.text:
@PhotonCap @jukan05 If the moonshot (which is massive price hikes on InP like HBM) fails, then $AXTI increases revenue from  capacity expansion from Northland's $100m raise and it becomes re-rated anyway. 

There's two separate bottlenecks, one is all the precursors and feedstock (which $AXTI / Vital owns, ~80% or so comes from China it's a physics problem). 

Then there's substrates, which as you know $AXTI, Sumitomo are leading. $JX, Vital ?, and $COHR are probably scaling. 

So its incredibly defensible that $AXTI owns both supply chains for upstream materials as well as substrate productions. 

InP will still be needed with companies like $POET, it just reduces usage by a considerable amount (and it's used for many other applications like lidar/drones, etc).

So aside from $POET, $ALMU maybe BTO as a material, don't think there's public companies with direct exposure yet. HyperLight for TFLN. Then $MRVL Inphi, and OpenLight (private). I'm sure $COHR, and all the others are working on a workaround too (none of them prob until 2028). 

But even with I say workarounds they still use inp in some manner, just less, and the amount of inp/module for rubin and networking is just massive so $AXTI capacity expansion should be fully absorbed.

Photonics is the next major bottleneck. 

$NVDA has signaled each one ahead of time from: 

HBM (with Samsung/Sk Hynix) to CoWoS and now with the $LITE and $COHR investment: 

Laser Fab, CPO, and InP. 

For the most asymmetrical longs in each bottleneck:

1. InP Substrates: $AXTI, Sumitomo, JX
2. InP Upstream Feedstock + Processing: $AXTI
3. Lasers: $AAOI (internal), $AVGO, $COHR, $LITE
4. CPO: $TSEM, Soitec. 

The laser bottleneck was confirmed from the $AAOI earnings call when three different hyperscaler wanted to buy out any optical transceiver they can produce. 

The InP substrate bottleneck was confirmed with the backlog from $AXTI. (Image source of players: IndexBox)

And the CPO bottleneck is widely expected to happen later in late 2027-2028. 

There's short term volatility from $AVGO comments around "CPO" in specific. But that's different than the laser -> transceiver and InP bottlenecks happening now. 

For timeframes: 
$AAOI, $LITE, $COHR and the laser transceiver bottleneck is happening real time (and is expected to get worse like memory into 2028).

$AXTI, Sumitomo and the InP substrate bottleneck is happening real time (and is expected to get worse as long as AI uses photonics for the many years to come).

And the larger architectural shift to CPO led by $NVDA will likely happen in 2028. 

These feel inevitable for the next paradigm shift in AI.

@Jornka329996 Oh it was Nippon Chemical Industries at $169m, if you take down that company it disrupts the entire AI buildout with photonics.

Since its the #1 supplier of red phosphorus needed to make inp substrates at $AXTI, Sumitomo, and JX.
